helpful: Provisioned petition.txt with the full QP and reasons-for-granting section made the no-split assessment possible without any PDF fetching.
Statpack relist-count and CVSG cuts gave a concrete sub-1% anchor for a zero-relist petition.
fedcourts query on recent grants gave a useful contrast profile (distribution_count, salience) for what granted petitions look like.
gaps: The statpack salience-band table referenced by the predict prompt is absent from the committed statpack.md, so the band-level yardstick could not be used.
No statpack cut for response-waived / no-CFR petitions, which is one of the strongest denial signals; had to apply it judgmentally.
CourtListener opinion search does not index unpublished CA9 memoranda, so the decision below could only be read via the petition's account.
notes: Forward-mode cell; retrieval stayed well under budget (1 corpus query, 3 MCP searches).
